挽.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 免 — your hand pulls something along or rolls up a sleeve.

sentence.en: He rolled up his sleeves to work.

捂.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 吾 — you use your hand to cover something up tightly.

sentence.en: She covered her mouth and laughed.

捅.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 甬 — the hand jabs or pokes at something.

sentence.en: He gave it a poke with his finger.

捆.origin: The hand radical 扌 next to 困 — your hands tie things up into a bundle.

sentence.en: He tied the books together.

捋.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 寽 — the hand strokes or pulls along, gathering as it goes.

sentence.en: Grandpa stroked his beard.

捌.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 别 — to split open by hand; it also writes the formal number eight.

sentence.en: The ticket says eight yuan.

捎.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 肖 — your hand carries a little something along for someone.

sentence.en: Please take something along for me.

捐.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 肙 — your hand gives money or things away to help others.

sentence.en: Everyone donated money to the disaster area.

捕.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 甫 — the hand reaches out to catch or seize something.

sentence.en: The fisherman catches fish in the river.

损.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 员 — careless hands can damage or harm things.

sentence.en: This book is damaged.

捡.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 佥 — your hand bends down to pick something up.

sentence.en: I picked up a pen.

捣.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 岛 — the hand pounds down again and again.

word.en: to make trouble, to be naughty

sentence.en: My little brother is good at making mischief.

捧.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 奉 — both hands held out together to offer something.

sentence.en: She held a cup of hot tea in both hands.

据.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 居 — to hold on to or rely on something.

sentence.en: It is said it will rain tomorrow.

捶.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 垂 — the hand strikes or pounds with a fist.

sentence.en: I pound Grandma's back for her.

捺.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 奈 — to press down firmly with the fingers; also a writing stroke.

sentence.en: This character has a falling-right stroke.

捻.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 念 — fingers twist and rub something into a thread.

sentence.en: Grandma twists thread with her fingers.

掀.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 欣 — the hand lifts or stirs something up.

sentence.en: The wind lifted the curtain.

掂.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 店 — you weigh something by hefting it in your hand.

word.en: to weigh in the hand, to size up

sentence.en: He hefted the stone in his hand.

授.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 受 — one hand gives and teaches, the other hand receives.

sentence.en: The teacher teaches very seriously.

掌.origin: 尚 over 手 (hand) — the palm of your hand, which is also what takes charge.

sentence.en: His palm is very big.

掏.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 匋 — the hand digs into a pocket to take something out.

word.en: to take out, to pull out

sentence.en: He took money out of his pocket.

掐.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 臽 — fingers pinch or nip something tightly.

sentence.en: Don't pinch me.

排.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 非 — hands line things up in a neat row.

sentence.en: The students line up to enter the classroom.

掖.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 夜 — the hand tucks or folds something in.

sentence.en: She tucked in the quilt neatly.

掘.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 屈 — hands dig down into the ground.

sentence.en: The workers are digging a well.

掠.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 京 — to grab and sweep away, or brush past quickly.

word.en: to sweep past, to brush by

sentence.en: A little bird swept past in the sky.

掣.origin: 制 over 手 (hand) — the hand pulls or drags something back.

sentence.en: The car sped away fast as lightning.

控.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 空 — to hold and keep something under your control.

sentence.en: He couldn't hold back his tears.

推.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 隹 — your hands push something forward.

sentence.en: He pushed the door open hard.

掩.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 奄 — the hand covers or hides something.

sentence.en: She covered her face with her hand.

掬.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 匊 — both hands cup together to scoop something up.

word.en: to scoop up water with the hands

sentence.en: He scooped up clear water with his hands.

掰.origin: 手 分 手 — two hands pulling apart what is in the middle, to break it in two.

sentence.en: He broke the cake into two halves.

掴.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 国 — the open hand slaps.

sentence.en: You must not slap others.

掷.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 郑 — the hand throws or hurls something away.

sentence.en: He hurled the stone hard.

掸.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 单 — the hand flicks dust away.

sentence.en: She dusted off the table.

掺.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 参 — your hand mixes one thing into another.

word.en: to mix in water, to dilute

sentence.en: Water was mixed into the milk.

掼.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 贯 — to fling or smash something to the ground.

word.en: to throw down, to fling down

sentence.en: He angrily flung down his backpack.

揉.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 柔 (soft) — gentle hands knead and rub.

sentence.en: He rubbed his eyes.

揍.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 奏 — the hand hits or beats someone.

sentence.en: We shouldn't hit people.

描.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 苗 — your hand traces lines to draw or describe.

sentence.en: He traced a little bird.

插.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 臿 — the hand sticks something into a slot.

sentence.en: She put the flowers into the vase.

揖.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 咠 — hands clasped together in a polite bow.

word.en: to make a bow with hands clasped

sentence.en: He bowed to the guest with clasped hands.

握.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 屋 — the hand closes to grasp and hold tight.

sentence.en: The two shook hands and greeted each other.

揣.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 耑 — to tuck something away inside your clothes.

word.en: to carry tucked in one's clothes

sentence.en: He has a letter tucked in his clothes.

揩.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 皆 — the hand wipes something clean.

sentence.en: Please wipe the table clean.

揪.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 秋 — fingers pinch and grab tightly.

sentence.en: He grabbed the corner of my clothes.

揭.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 曷 — the hand lifts a cover or peels something off.

sentence.en: He lifted off the pot lid.

揽.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 览 — arms reach out to gather or embrace.

sentence.en: Mom held me in her arms.

搀.origin: The hand radical 扌 plus 毚 — a hand supports someone, or mixes things together.

sentence.en: He helped the old man across the road.
